materialChanges: Clarified the fixed-caliper misconception prompt so it asks about systematic measurement offset, states the two means are each shifted by 2 mm relative to their own specimens, and separates bias from sampling precision.

Added scoped claims for sample selection versus random assignment and repeatability versus measurement validity, mapped them to the opening scene, and expanded that scene's learner-visible source note to cover their complete supporting-source union.
Recorded the published correction DOI 10.1093/aje/kwac129 and PMID 36205044 in the misclassification source record, including the corrected multilevel-exposure example and associated table material.

Aligned ATTRIBUTION.md to lesson version 0.1.1, replaced the stale prior-candidate governance account with the current candidate and eligible review set, and disclosed the fresh finalizer run with runtime-stamped identity placeholders.
Repaired the practice scene's skipped heading level so its retrieval feedback is a level-two section in the semantic outline.

reviewDispositions.rationale: Rewrote the caliper prompt to ask which laboratory has the smaller systematic offset and answered 'neither'; the explanation now distinguishes the invariant 2 mm measurement bias from the larger sample's possible reduction in sampling fluctuation.
Added claim-sampling-versus-assignment and claim-repeatability-versus-validity, mapped both to scene-find-path, and named them with their complete source union in the same-scene source note.
Updated source-misclassification-direction to disclose the published correction DOI and PMID and to identify the multilevel-exposure example and related table material corrected.

This affirmative note confirms the worked seedling arithmetic and estimand boundary. I independently retained 80% versus 70% among survivors, 72% versus 49% among all assigned seedlings, the respective 10- and 23-percentage-point contrasts, and unresolved observer-bias direction.

This affirmative note confirms the keyed acoustic-classifier transfer audit. The final assessment retains stage-matched mechanisms and checks, unequal-denominator analysis, the selective-reporting/publication distinction, and calibrated uncertainty about direction.
Updated ATTRIBUTION.md from version 0.1.0 to 0.1.1 and also replaced its stale earlier-candidate governance statement with the current candidate and review-input record.
This affirmative first-read finding requires no repair. The concrete fictional seedling result remains before the formal bias definition, scope, and objectives.

This affirmative coherence finding requires no repair. The final lesson retains the evidence pipeline, overlapping-category boundaries, and direction cautions without forcing mutually exclusive labels.
This affirmative worked-example finding requires no repair. The final seedling audit retains task representation, plan, execution, calculation check, interpretation, and self-explanation prompt.

The reviewer confirms that version 0.1.1 already corrected the older glossary scene mappings and broadened the random-error definition appropriately; the finalizer verified the current glossary record and made no further change.
This affirmative transfer finding requires no repair. The acoustic-classifier case remains a novel bounded context requiring selection and adaptation of the evidence-path audit.

evidenceChecked: Checked the manifest's six-scene semantic reading order, one level-one heading per scene, and heading hierarchy; repaired the sole skipped level in the practice scene.

Checked the declarative evidence-pipeline diagram, its concise alt text, outcome-equivalent long description, directed node-and-edge record, and complete numbered prose alternative; no learning distinction depends only on color or position.

Checked that every prompt, response, feedback route, retry, and transfer task can be completed in text without drag, hover, sound, motion, timing, pointer precision, personal disclosure, or a physical procedure; the assessment is untimed and retakeable.

Checked plain semantic Markdown and local JSON for reflow and keyboard-compatible source order; found no raw HTML, scripts, iframes, Markdown images, remote embeds, active SVG, unsafe URL schemes, executable notebooks, or fixed-layout interaction requirements.
Checked all ten final claims against valid scene and source IDs and verified that each mapped scene exposes the claim and the complete supporting-source union through learner-visible source notes.

Checked all nine source records for factual-reference use, facts-only original-expression rights basis and evidence, dated checked-no-agent-restriction-found status, HTTPS terms route, locator, and source licence disclosure; no source is marked human-only.

Checked ATTRIBUTION.md, lesson.json, and the rights ledger for the CC BY 4.0 contributor grant, accountable principal, author-agent disclosures, finalizer disclosure, original-synthesis statement, modifications, and separation of evidence from expression reuse.

Checked lesson.json.thirdPartyAssets and the pack contents: there are no third-party images, media, datasets, or redistributed source expression; the only diagram and all cases, prompts, tables, calculations, and assessments are declared original course material.

Checked the fictional-case labels, educational boundary, absence of identifiable learner or patient information, absence of clinical advice or credential claims, and absence of instructions for diagnosis, treatment, ingestion, self-experiment, or unsafe physical work.
limitations: The audit is a static source audit; no browser renderer, screen reader, 200% zoom session, print export, reduced-motion mode, or assistive-technology user test was available in this run.
The operator prohibited reading site/, so the finalizer did not directly inspect the public adjudication schema, review schema, quorum JSON, renderer implementation, or course terminology ledger.

The operator prohibited npm commands, so strict source preflight and canonical repository validation were not run; JSON parsing, cross-reference, semantic-heading, security-pattern, rights-ledger, and diff checks were run instead.

The GitHub plugin was not installed, so the stated COMMENTED transports on pull request 53 and their non-dismissed state were not independently queried; eligibility relies on the committed artifacts and the operator-supplied transport facts.

Source-access terms and source content were not re-fetched during finalization; the audit checked the dated access attestations, factual-use records, review evidence, and disclosed published correction in the pack.

rationale: The original candidate is a coherent, accurate introductory lesson with correct seedling calculations, aligned assessment logic, explicit uncertainty, genuine transfer, complete source-note mappings, original reusable expression, and no third-party assets. The two counted current-version reviews target candidate e270afdba6c4aa1a21172886c8f810b96a338fda, provide the required academic and learning-design roles, record approve verdicts, and declare distinct run IDs and two declared model families/providers under one accountable principal. The two retained version 0.1.0 reviews target candidate 9df98b58e534225725314047d88538235159bd88 and are excluded. All eleven findings in the counted reviews are disposed: four minor findings were incorporated and seven affirmative notes required no change. The finalizer also repaired one skipped heading level found during the final accessibility audit. The artifact-level review-input snapshot is satisfied based on the committed records and operator-supplied non-dismissed COMMENTED transport facts; this is disclosed and attested governance, not proof of independent agents, providers, or GitHub state. The final static academic, assessment, accessibility, rights, privacy, security, and cross-reference checks found no unresolved content blocker. Merge is therefore supported once the operator creates and substitutes the real final content commit, stamps runtime identity, and completes the reserved canonical validation and PR-provenance checks.
conditions: The operator must commit the final lesson revision, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character SHA, and stamp all four RUNTIME-STAMPED adjudicator identity fields from the runtime banner before submitting the adjudication.

Before merge, the operator must run the strict source preflight and canonical repository validation and confirm the adjudication schema, commit ordering, final-content immutability, and equivalent non-dismissed GitHub review transports; any failure blocks merge until corrected within maintainer authority.

After finalization, the maintainer may perform only the separately permitted publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields; no teaching-content change is authorized by this decision.
